description: Provenance recorder identity derivation (normative since @veritio/core 0.4.2; see spec/provenance-identity.md). Record-method edges are OCCURRENCE-SCOPED by their owning event id (edge_<ownerEventId>__<fromType>:<fromId>__<relation>__<toType>:<toId>) so re-recording the same logical link under a new event never collides on the per-tenant idempotency key; link() keeps endpoint-only SINGLETON ids (edge_<fromType>:<fromId>__<relation>__<toType>:<toId>). Prompt event ids append the occurrence time (normalized via Date.toISOString(): UTC, milliseconds) when occurredAt is supplied, so the same prompt text twice in one session yields two occurrences while identical replays keep identical ids. Any SDK that ships a provenance recorder MUST reproduce these derivations byte-for-byte (Python/Go recorders are a parity TODO; this fixture is their contract).

edgeCases.name: link() singleton edge keeps endpoint-only identity
record-method edge is scoped by its owning event id
same endpoints under a later owning event yield a DISTINCT id (no idempotency-key collision)

promptEventCases.name: prompt without occurredAt keeps the constant (session, promptHash) id
prompt with occurredAt appends the normalized UTC ISO instant (milliseconds, Z)
offset timestamps normalize to the same UTC instant and therefore the same id

defaultEventIdCases.name: file-change event id derives from source tree and result version when the caller supplies no id
